score_note: Explains cell/room/door cardinalities, but states the dominance quantification imprecisely.
Correctly connects the mini-image characterization of sigma with later cardinality comparisons.
Correctly distinguishes point insertion and index insertion, but does not explain incidence counting.
Definitions are right, but the fixed-type parity-counting role is still only generic.
Explains that colorful cells must be rooms before point extraction, but misses the nonempty/cardinality-sandwich argument.
Accurately explains typed door-room incidence and the odd/even partition.
Mentions cardinality one and oddness, but omits the fixed type and singleton outside-door construction.
Identifies parity as the key but does not spell out the odd + even = colorful + even equation.
States colorful existence, but gives the wrong reason for needing Inhabited I/default index.
Correctly explains Fcolor, Scarf at each grid level, room_seq, and room_point_seq extraction.
Understands finite-image subsequence extraction, but confuses the stabilized room color set with a constant point color.
Correctly notes coordinates outside C vanish, but misses how this combines with inequalities to force equality.
Mentions compactness and vanishing diameter, but misses the transfer-of-inequalities role.
Mentions coordinate inequality and continuity, but misses colored witnesses and vanishing-diameter transfer.
Gets the high-level sequence/subsequence structure but misses diameter estimates and the final sum argument.
Understands pushing toward uniformity and normalization, but misses the explicit positive-block-sum/denominator issue.
Identifies project-embed identity, but overstates it as a bijection and does not explain fixed-point conversion.
Mentions lifted map and continuity, but incorrectly describes where the Brouwer fixed point lives.
Explains FinGame, mixedS, and expected payoff, but does not spell out the no-profitable-deviation condition.
Explains normalization and continuity generally, but misses the payoff-improvement role of g_function.
Correct high-level connection, but misses pure-deviation contradiction and mixed_g_linear extension.
Correctly identifies internal doors as having exactly two incident rooms, giving fiber size two and evenness.
Correctly explains the same-missing-type versus colorful dichotomy for typed door-room incidences.
Gets the two-element fiber and evenness, but does not explicitly explain the two nearly-colorful doors mechanism.
Correctly explains TT grids, normalization into the simplex, and TT.ILO as the order structure for Scarf.
Mentions coordinate bounds and diameter shrinkage, but misses rescaling and outside-C vanishing details.
Correct about extracting a constant color-set subsequence, but does not connect it to outside-C and in-C limiting estimates.
Names the final lemmas and extensionality, but explains the outside-C zero and sum-forcing argument imprecisely.
Explains flat versus block indexing, but misses inverse lemmas and sum/retraction uses.
Identifies blockWeight scaling, sum-one, and zero deficit, but misses projection recovery via project_embed_id.
Correctly explains normalized division, denominator continuity, and positivity/nonzero denominator for continuity.
Understands the case split, but misses the zero-deficit argument when tPush is zero.
Correctly explains mixed_g_linear as a weighted pure-deviation expansion used to bound mixed deviations by best pure deviations.
Gets transport to Fin-indexed product simplices, but omits the pullback of the fixed point to the original mixed space.
States that nash_map_cert gives a simplex point, but misses nonnegativity, denominator positivity, and sum-one details.
Identifies the sum_g = 1 contradiction, but omits sum_g > 1 from profitable deviation and the fixedness/positive-coordinate mechanism.
